rulings.Case Title: DCFS TRUST VS. OSCAR H. MARTINEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Amendment Of Judgment; Declaration Of David T. Chen In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 17, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DCFS TRUST'S Motion For Amendment Of Judgment. Denied without prejudice. Judgment debtor Oscar H. Martinez was not served. The documents attached to Chen Declaration as Exhibit 5 suggest Elvira Martinez may not be have been served with this motion at the correct address. Further, there is insufficient evidence in the Chen Declaration to establish that Elvira Y Martinez also goes by the name Elvira Velasquez. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE BECK VS. TONG HO HSING INDUSTRIAL CO LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Quash Subpoena And For Protective Order For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 17, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BECK Ntc Of Motion To Quash Subpoena And For Protective Order For Production Of Business Records Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion; moving party shall submit a revised proposed order which deletes all of p. 2 except lines 1-3. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA L. PETERSON VS. US BANCORP DBA US BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 17, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT U.S. BANCORP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden by showing that it did not issue and had no involvement in Plaintiff's credit card account. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact in response. Plaintiff's request for continuance is denied. Plaintiff fails to state any discovery that is necessary to oppose the motion or state why such facts could not be discovered earlier. CCP §437(c)(h). The motion was filed on June 29, 2012, and has already been continued. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437(c)(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAKE MACLEOD-ROEMER VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For A Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 17, 2012, Line 6, PETITIONER JAKE MACLEOD-ROEMER'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus. The petition for writ of mandate is granted. Plaintiff demonstrated just cause for relief. Respondent acted arbitrarily and without substantial reason when it denied petitioner's request for relief from the deadline. Petitioner may file a motion for fees per Gov't Code § 800(a). Petitioner shall prepare a statement of decision in compliance with CCP 632. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL KUTE VS. CHEVRONTEXACO OIL CO., A DELAWARE CORP.
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining That Pltf Is A Vexatious Litigant And Requiring That Pltf Deposit Security
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 17, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT CHEVRON CORPORATION, CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'S (Amended) Motion For Order Determining That Plaintiff Is A Vexatious Litigant And Requiring That Plaintiff Deposit Security. Granted. Defendants have shown that Plaintiff is a vexatious litigant under Code of Civil Procedure sections 391(b)(1) and 391(b)(3). It is not reasonably probable that Plaintiff will prevail in this case. Plaintiff is ordered to post a bond of $10,000 within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
